summary: A review of two biographies of Hypatia of Alexandria, detailing her historical context, mathematical achievements, and brutal murder. The text notes that almost none of her original writing survives, save for one reported quote used to shock a student out of physical lust and into philosophical awareness.

principles.statement: True beauty is distinct from base physical attraction.
principles.rationale: To shock a student out of physical lust and guide him toward philosophical awareness, demonstrating that the physical body is tied to base, unbeautiful realities.
principles.source_quote: It is this you love, young man, not beauty

principles.statement: The deepest mysteries of philosophy are not accessible to the general public.
principles.rationale: Philosophy requires an elevated understanding that the masses (hoi polloi) lack, though this elitist attitude did not prevent her from giving public lectures.

signature_quotes.text: It is this you love, young man, not beauty
signature_quotes.context: Spoken while hurling a soiled sanitary napkin at a student to arrest his amorous advances and shock him into philosophical awareness.

anti_patterns.description: Pursuing physical lust under the guise of loving beauty.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Physical bodies are tied to base, unbeautiful realities, whereas true philosophical love should be directed toward higher, non-physical beauty.
